gaps.title: Tenant isolation bypass in billing API
gaps.breaks: One tenant can access another tenant's subscription details
gaps.scenario: Tenant A makes request to GET /api/billing/subscription with company_id=2 and sees Tenant B's plan details, pricing, and payment information
gaps.prompt: Write a test that attempts to access billing subscription details for different company IDs without proper authorization. Verify that each request returns 403 Forbidden when accessing company_id that doesn't match the authenticated user's company. Include test cases for both valid and invalid company_id parameters in the request.

gaps.title: Race condition in subscription upgrade
gaps.breaks: Multiple simultaneous upgrade requests result in incorrect billing state
gaps.scenario: Tenant with 2K tickets sends two concurrent upgrade requests from Starter to Growth plan. Both succeed, but they're only charged once while getting double the ticket allowance, or they're charged twice but only get the single upgrade benefit
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates concurrent subscription upgrade requests. Send multiple simultaneous POST /api/billing/upgrade requests for the same tenant. Verify that only one upgrade is processed, the correct amount is charged exactly once, and the subscription state is consistent. Test with different time intervals between requests (0ms, 100ms, 500ms).

gaps.title: Missing idempotency for upgrade/cancel endpoints
gaps.breaks: Duplicate requests cause duplicate charges or multiple state changes
gaps.scenario: Payment webhook double-fires after a successful upgrade, causing the same upgrade request to be processed twice, resulting in the customer being charged twice for the same plan change
gaps.prompt: Write a test that sends identical POST /api/billing/upgrade and POST /api/billing/cancel requests multiple times with the same parameters. Verify that subsequent requests after the first successful one return the same result without making additional changes or charges. Test with and without proper idempotency headers.

gaps.title: Frontend state inconsistency after API failure
gaps.breaks: UI shows incorrect subscription status after failed backend operation
gaps.scenario: User clicks "Upgrade" button, API call fails with a 500 error, but frontend doesn't properly handle the error and still shows "Upgrading..." spinner or displays the upgraded plan despite the failure
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates API failures for billing operations (upgrade, cancel) and verifies that the frontend properly handles these errors. Test scenarios: 500 server errors, network timeouts, and partial failures where some operations succeed but others don't. Ensure the UI shows appropriate error messages and maintains the correct state.

gaps.title: Missing rollback on partial upgrade failure
gaps.breaks: Incomplete upgrade leaves system in inconsistent state
gaps.scenario: During upgrade from Starter to Growth plan, the system successfully updates the subscription tier in the database but fails to process the payment with Paddle, leaving the tenant in a partially upgraded state with no way to access the higher tier features
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates partial failures in the upgrade process. Mock scenarios where the database update succeeds but the payment processing fails, or vice versa. Verify that the system implements proper rollback mechanisms to restore the original subscription state if any part of the upgrade process fails.

raw_response:
GAPS FOUND: 5

GAP 1
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Tenant isolation bypass in billing API
What breaks: One tenant can access another tenant's subscription details
Real scenario: Tenant A makes request to GET /api/billing/subscription with company_id=2 and sees Tenant B's plan details, pricing, and payment information
AI agent prompt: Write a test that attempts to access billing subscription details for different company IDs without proper authorization. Verify that each request returns 403 Forbidden when accessing company_id that doesn't match the authenticated user's company. Include test cases for both valid and invalid company_id parameters in the request.

GAP 2
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Race condition in subscription upgrade
What breaks: Multiple simultaneous upgrade requests result in incorrect billing state
Real scenario: Tenant with 2K tickets sends two concurrent upgrade requests from Starter to Growth plan. Both succeed, but they're only charged once while getting double the ticket allowance, or they're charged twice but only get the single upgrade benefit
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates concurrent subscription upgrade requests. Send multiple simultaneous POST /api/billing/upgrade requests for the same tenant. Verify that only one upgrade is processed, the correct amount is charged exactly once, and the subscription state is consistent. Test with different time intervals between requests (0ms, 100ms, 500ms).

GAP 3
Severity: HIGH
Title: Missing idempotency for upgrade/cancel endpoints
What breaks: Duplicate requests cause duplicate charges or multiple state changes
Real scenario: Payment webhook double-fires after a successful upgrade, causing the same upgrade request to be processed twice, resulting in the customer being charged twice for the same plan change
AI agent prompt: Write a test that sends identical POST /api/billing/upgrade and POST /api/billing/cancel requests multiple times with the same parameters. Verify that subsequent requests after the first successful one return the same result without making additional changes or charges. Test with and without proper idempotency headers.

GAP 4
Severity: HIGH
Title: Frontend state inconsistency after API failure
What breaks: UI shows incorrect subscription status after failed backend operation
Real scenario: User clicks "Upgrade" button, API call fails with a 500 error, but frontend doesn't properly handle the error and still shows "Upgrading..." spinner or displays the upgraded plan despite the failure
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates API failures for billing operations (upgrade, cancel) and verifies that the frontend properly handles these errors. Test scenarios: 500 server errors, network timeouts, and partial failures where some operations succeed but others don't. Ensure the UI shows appropriate error messages and maintains the correct state.

GAP 5
Severity: MEDIUM
Title: Missing rollback on partial upgrade failure
What breaks: Incomplete upgrade leaves system in inconsistent state
Real scenario: During upgrade from Starter to Growth plan, the system successfully updates the subscription tier in the database but fails to process the payment with Paddle, leaving the tenant in a partially upgraded state with no way to access the higher tier features
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates partial failures in the upgrade process. Mock scenarios where the database update succeeds but the payment processing fails, or vice versa. Verify that the system implements proper rollback mechanisms to restore the original subscription state if any part of the upgrade process fails.
